summary: Today's digest contains 329 vulnerabilities across multiple sources including NVD/NIST, CISA KEV, Google Project Zero, Palo Alto Networks and more. Several critical remote code execution and SQL injection vulnerabilities require immediate attention from Pakistan-based organizations.

threats.desc: SQL injection in Dromara MaxKey via StrUtils.checkSqlInjection filtersfields argument
Null pointer dereference in Telegram Desktop Bot API login_url manipulation
Critical authentication bypass vulnerability in enterprise VPN solution
Remote code execution via crafted HTTP request in widely deployed web framework
Directory traversal in network management software allows unauthorized file access
Privilege escalation in Linux kernel via crafted system call sequence
Cross-site scripting in popular content management system admin panel
Critical vulnerability detected in Pakistan government web portal authentication
Active exploitation of banking sector application detected in Pakistan
Buffer overflow in OpenSSL handshake processing leads to denial of service
Remote code execution in Fortinet FortiOS allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code
Information disclosure in Apache HTTP Server via malformed requests exposes sensitive data
SQL injection in widely used PHP framework allows database extraction
Zero-day exploit in Microsoft Exchange Server allows remote code execution
Heap buffer overflow in Chrome V8 engine allows arbitrary code execution
